clauses.text: Confidentiality Agreement dated February 6, 2026.
The merger has been approved by the board of Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. and by the Board of Managers of Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC; neither approval is described as unanimous.
Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. stockholders are entitled to receive $21.50 in cash per share of Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. common stock.
Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. is not permitted to pay dividends prior to closing, except with Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC’s prior written consent.
Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC’s dividend rights prior to closing are not addressed in the excerpts.
Subject to no stockholder votes or consents and adoption by sole stockholder of Merger Sub.
Closing is conditioned on expiration or termination of the HSR Act waiting period and all other required antitrust approvals.
Committed financing was not included in the merger contract.
Outside Date is December 6, 2026; automatically extends to May 5, 2027 upon specified Exhibit A satisfaction/waiver.

Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC has no obligation to accept any structural or behavioral antitrust remedies (no divestitures, holds-separate, contract changes, or conduct commitments). HSR filing due within 15 business days; CFIUS not addressed in excerpts.
Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC shall use reasonable best efforts to defend or contest Proceedings challenging the Agreement or Transactions, seeking vacatur or reversal of stays or injunctions.

Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC controls Governmental Entity strategy/timing (after consultation with Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc.); no HSR/Foreign Antitrust waiting-period extension or delay agreement without other’s consent; may pull/refile once unilaterally.

Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC controls regulatory-approval strategy and Governmental Entity interactions after consultation with Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc.; any waiting-period extension/timing agreement requires mutual written consent, except Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC may pull-and-refile once per jurisdiction unilaterally.

The excerpts do not address an FTC warning letter; they only require Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC and Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. to coordinate HSR/antitrust filings and communications, without stating any closing-condition impact.

Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. may, during the Pre-Closing Period, respond to a Qualifying Company Takeover Proposal by signing an Acceptable Confidentiality Agreement, sharing information (with Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC within 24 hours), and negotiating (including soliciting revisions), only if the Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. Board makes required good-faith fiduciary determinations and gives written notice identifying the bidder.
Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. owes Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC $87,700,000 upon superior proposal termination.
No reimbursement fees apply for failure to obtain shareholder approval.

No reimbursement fees apply for failure to obtain shareholder approval. Section 7.04 only requires Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC to pay Indemnified Parties’ enforcement expenses, not termination/vote-failure reimbursements.

Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. shall, and shall cause the Company Subsidiaries to, conduct its business in the ordinary course and use commercially reasonable efforts to (x) preserve intact its present business organization, (y) keep available the services of its present employees and (z) preserve its present relationships and goodwill with suppliers, licensors, licensees, contractors, customers, partners and others having material business dealings with it.

MAE carve-outs appear standard and include cyber-intrusion as a specified force majeure-type event, plus carve-out limitations tied to the Company Disclosure Letter, Section 4.05, Exhibit A, and Section 6.01.
Specific performance is available to Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC, Servier Detroit Inc., Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc., and Servier S.A.S.
This Agreement shall be governed by the Laws of Delaware.
Proceedings relating to this Agreement shall be heard and determined exclusively in the Delaware Court of Chancery.
Company is Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc., a Delaware corporation.
Acquirer is Servier Detroit Inc., a Delaware corporation.

Transaction structure: Reverse triangular merger. Surviving entity: Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. Supporting language: “Servier Detroit Inc. … shall be merged with and into Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc.… [and] Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. shall continue as the surviving corporation.”
Servier S.A.S., a French société par actions simplifiée; Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC is its indirect wholly-owned subsidiary.

Servier Detroit Inc. is a Delaware corporation and a wholly owned subsidiary of Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC. The excerpts do not provide its incorporation date or any other relationship details beyond wholly owned status.
The merger has been approved by the board of directors of Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. and by the board of managers of Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC; neither approval is described as unanimous.

Merger Closing occurs on a date set by Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC and Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc., ASAP after Offer Closing Time, no later than second Business Day after Article VIII conditions satisfied/waived.
Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. stockholders are entitled to receive $21.50 in cash per share of Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. common stock.

Yes—contingent payments exist: at the Effective Time, Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. stock options with an exercise price below the Merger Consideration are cashed out for the excess per underlying share, Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. RSUs are canceled for cash equal to the Merger Consideration per underlying share, and outstanding Pre-Funded Warrants become exercisable solely for the same Merger Consideration the holder would have received as a stockholder.
Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. may not pay dividends prior to closing without Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC’s prior written consent.
Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC’s ability to pay dividends prior to closing is not addressed in the provided excerpts.
If, between March 6, 2026 and the Effective Time, Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. effects a stock split/dividend or similar transaction, the $21.50 Merger Consideration is appropriately adjusted.

Mutual conditions include: (i) the absence of any Judgment, other legal restraint or prohibition imposed by any Governmental Entity of competent jurisdiction, or any Law, that prevents or prohibits consummation of the Merger of Servier Detroit Inc. with and into Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc.; and (ii) Servier Detroit Inc. having accepted or caused to be irrevocably accepted for payment all shares of Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. common stock validly tendered and not properly withdrawn pursuant to the Offer.

Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC’s obligation (and the other parties’ obligation) to consummate the merger is conditioned on (i) the absence of any governmental judgment, law, or other legal restraint prohibiting the merger and (ii) Servier Detroit Inc. having accepted (or caused to be irrevocably accepted for payment) all shares of Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. common stock validly tendered and not properly withdrawn in the cash tender offer.
The agreement contains no specified conditions precedent to the Company’s obligation to consummate the closing (i.e., no express “Company Closing Conditions” are set forth).

Subject to assuming the Transactions are consummated in accordance with Section 251(h) of the DGCL and assuming the accuracy of the representations and warranties set forth in Section 5.08, no stockholder votes or consents are needed to authorize or adopt this Agreement or for consummation of the Transactions.
As promptly as practicable, the parties shall jointly prepare and file the Joint Proxy Statement/Prospectus in preliminary form and Parent shall prepare and file a Registration Statement on Form F-4.

No proxy statement/Form F-4 efforts standard or timing deadlines are stated in the excerpts; Section 7.02(a) only states Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC, Servier Detroit Inc., and Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. “use its reasonable best efforts.”

Assuming the Transactions are consummated in accordance with Section 251(h) of the DGCL and assuming the accuracy of the representations and warranties set forth in Section 5.08, no stockholder votes or consents are needed.

Vote may be delayed no more than 25 Business Days in the aggregate if insufficient affirmative votes for quorum or Company Stockholder Approval; adjournments no longer than 10 Business Days each, not exceeding 20 Business Days aggregate.

Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC, Servier Detroit Inc. and Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. agree to take all necessary and appropriate action to cause the Merger to become, and that the Merger shall become, effective as soon as practicable following the consummation of the Offer, without a vote.
Appraisal Shares under DGCL Section 262 not converted into Merger Consideration; Section 262 applies.
Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC, Servier Detroit Inc. and Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. shall use RBE to secure HSR/foreign antitrust clearances ASAP, by the Outside Date.

Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC and Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. must file HSR Notification and Report Forms with the FTC/DOJ ASAP, no later than 15 BD post-Agreement Date (subject to timely information).

Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC and Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. must file all appropriate filings, notices, applications or similar documents required under applicable Foreign Antitrust Laws promptly.

Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC (as “Parent”) and Servier Detroit Inc. (as “Merger Sub”) must use “reasonable best efforts to avoid, eliminate or resolve each and every impediment and obtain all clearances, consents, approvals and waivers under the HSR Act or any Foreign Antitrust Law,” but neither Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC, Servier Detroit Inc., nor any of their respective Affiliates (including after closing Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. and its subsidiaries) is required to “(i) sell, license, divest or dispose of or hold separate any assets, Intellectual Property or businesses,” “(ii) terminate, amend or assign” contracts, “(iii) change or modify any course of conduct regarding future operations,” “(iv) take any other action with respect to its businesses, assets or rights,” or “(v) commit to take any such action.” Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. cannot, without Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC’s prior written consent, “propose, negotiate, commit to, agree to or consent to any divestiture, sale, disposition, hold separate order or other structural or conduct relief, or other operational undertakings” to obtain HSR/foreign antitrust clearance; no divestiture dollar/revenue cap is stated.

Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC controls regulatory/antitrust strategy: after consultation with Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc., giving due consideration to its views, and acting reasonably and in good faith, it directs all matters with any Governmental Entity and implements the approvals strategy; no waiting-period extension or “delay” agreement without the other party’s prior written consent, except Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC may pull-and-refile once per regime unilaterally.

Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC controls timing in regulatory approval processes by directing Governmental Entity interactions; however, neither Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC nor Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. may extend HSR/Foreign Antitrust waiting periods or enter delay agreements without the other’s prior written consent, except Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC may pull-and-refile once per jurisdiction in its sole discretion.
Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC, Servier Detroit Inc., and Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. are required to defend against any litigation.

Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. must use reasonable best efforts to prevent takeover proposals and related negotiations, with limited fiduciary-out exceptions requiring written notice to Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC; no regulatory best-efforts or addressed-letter/closing-condition procedure appears.

The agreement does not specifically address an FTC warning letter; it only requires Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC and Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. to share and coordinate FTC communications, and is silent on closing-condition impact.
Confidentiality Agreement dated February 6, 2026.

Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. may “(A) enter into an Acceptable Confidentiality Agreement…provide a copy…to Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC…(B) furnish information…within 24 hours…provides Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC…(C) participate in discussions…(including soliciting…).” “provided that” only “in response to a Company Takeover Proposal…Qualifying Company Takeover Proposal,” with “written notice to Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC…together with the identity.”

Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. shall, as promptly as reasonably practicable and in any event within 24 hours after receipt or exchange thereof, notify Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC in writing and provide material terms and conditions.

“Commencing upon the provision of any notice or materials referred to in the previous sentence, Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. and its Representatives shall keep Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC informed on a reasonably prompt basis (and in any event within 24 hours after receipt thereof) as to any material amendments or modifications with respect to any such Company Takeover Proposal or request, inquiry, proposal or offer (including any subsequent amendments or modifications thereto), including by furnishing any material amendments or modifications to materials previously provided under the preceding sentence.”

Prior to the Offer Closing Time, if Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. Board of Directors receives a Superior Company Proposal that did not result from a material breach of Section 6.02, Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. may make an Adverse Recommendation Change in response to the Superior Company Proposal, and may terminate this Agreement pursuant to Section 9.01(g) in order to enter into a definitive agreement with respect to the Superior Company Proposal, if Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. Board of Directors determines, in good faith, after consultation with outside counsel, that the failure to take such action would be inconsistent with its fiduciary duties under applicable Law.

Notwithstanding anything to contrary in the foregoing or any other provision of this Agreement, prior to the Offer Closing Time, (x) Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. Board of Directors may, in response to an Intervening Event, take any of the actions specified in clauses (A) or (D) of the definition of Adverse Recommendation Change (an "Intervening Event Adverse Recommendation Change") if the Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. Board of Directors determines, in good faith, after consultation with outside counsel, that the failure to take such action would be inconsistent with its fiduciary duties under applicable Law.
Committed financing was not included in the merger contract.

Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC and Servier Detroit Inc., jointly and severally, represent they “have…all of the funds necessary” through the Offer Closing Time/Effective Time, and closing isn’t conditioned on “ability to obtain financing.”

Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC and Servier Detroit Inc. jointly and severally represent they “will have…all of the funds necessary” through closing to buy shares, pay fees/expenses and amounts under Sections 3.10–3.11, and their obligations are not conditioned on “ability to obtain financing.”

Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC and Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. may terminate if the Offer Closing Time has not occurred by December 6, 2026. The Outside Date automatically extends to May 5, 2027 if, on December 6, 2026, specified Exhibit A Offer conditions (excluding listed exceptions) are satisfied or waived; no party action is required.

provided that the right to terminate this Agreement pursuant to this Section 9.01(b)(i) shall not be available to any party hereto if the failure of the Offer Closing Time to occur on or before the Outside Date is primarily due to a material breach of this Agreement by such party;

Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC and Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. may terminate this Agreement at any time prior to the Offer Closing Time (a) by mutual written consent of Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC, Servier Detroit Inc. and Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc., (b) by either Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC or Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. if the Offer Closing Time has not occurred by 11:59 p.m. (ET) on December 6, 2026 (automatically extended 150 days if specified Offer conditions are satisfied/waived), subject to breach-based limitations, or (c) by either if a final, non-appealable Legal Restraint permanently prohibits the Offer or Merger, subject to Section 7.02 compliance limitations.

Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC may terminate this Agreement pursuant to Section 9.01(c) if Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. breaches or fails to perform any representation, warranty, covenant or obligation and such breach/failure, individually or in the aggregate, (i) would result in the failure of any of the conditions in clauses (ii), (iii) or (iv) of Exhibit A and (ii) cannot be or has not been cured by the earlier of 30 days after written notice to Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. and the Outside Date, provided Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC and Servier Detroit Inc. are not then in material breach; Section 9.01(d) if an Adverse Recommendation Change has occurred; or Section 9.01(h) if the Offer (as extended under Section 2.01(a)) expires or is terminated/withdrawn in accordance with this Agreement without all Offer Conditions being satisfied or waived, subject to the extension and Parent-performance limitations in clauses (1) and (2) thereof.

Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. may terminate this Agreement pursuant to Section 9.01(e) if Servier Detroit Inc. (i) fails to commence the Offer in violation of Section 2.01 (other than due to Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc.’s violation of Section 2.02(b)), (ii) terminates the Offer prior to its expiration date (as extended under Section 2.01(a)) other than in accordance with this Agreement, or (iii) is obligated under Section 2.01(a) to irrevocably accept for payment and pay for shares, all Offer Conditions are satisfied or waived immediately prior to Offer expiration, and the Offer Closing Time does not occur within five Business Days after expiration; pursuant to Section 9.01(f) if Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC or Servier Detroit Inc. breaches/fails to perform (disregarding materiality/Parent MAE qualifiers) causing or reasonably expected to cause a Parent Material Adverse Effect and not cured by the earlier of 30 days after notice or the Outside Date (provided Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. is not then in material breach); pursuant to Section 9.01(g) to enter a definitive agreement for a Superior Company Proposal after complying with Section 6.02(b) and simultaneously paying the $87,700,000 Company Termination Fee under Section 9.03(a); or pursuant to Section 9.01(i) if the Offer (as extended under Section 2.01(a)) expires/is terminated/withdrawn in accordance with this Agreement without all Offer Conditions satisfied or waived, subject to Parent’s extension right under Section 2.01(a) and unavailability where the failed condition is primarily due to Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc.’s material nonperformance.

No reimbursement fees apply for failure to obtain shareholder approval. The only explicit reimbursement is that Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. must reimburse Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC for reasonable enforcement costs (plus prime-rate interest) only if Day One fails to timely pay the $87,700,000 termination fee and Servier obtains a judgment.
$87,700,000 – superior offer

Company Termination Fee of $87,700,000 is payable if Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. terminates the agreement to enter into a definitive agreement for a Superior Company Proposal, or if Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC terminates because an Adverse Recommendation Change has occurred, or if (after a takeover proposal arises and is not withdrawn) the agreement is terminated in specified ways and within 12 months Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. signs or completes a takeover deal.
$87,700,000 – superior offer

Parent Termination Fee of $87,700,000 is payable if Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. must pay Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC the “Company Termination Fee” because (1) Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. terminates to enter a Superior Company Proposal, (2) Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC terminates after an Adverse Recommendation Change, or (3) after a takeover proposal arises, the deal is terminated in specified ways and within 12 months Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. signs or closes that takeover deal.

Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. shall pay Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC a $87,700,000 Company Termination Fee if: (i) Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. terminates pursuant to Section 9.01(g) (payable prior to or simultaneously with termination); (ii) Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC terminates pursuant to Section 9.01(d) (payable within two Business Days after termination); or (iii) after the Agreement Date a bona fide Company Takeover Proposal (as modified for Section 9.03(a)(iii) by deeming 15% references to be 50%) is proposed/announced or becomes known to the Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. board and is not withdrawn by the specified timeframes, the Agreement is terminated under Section 9.01(b)(i), 9.01(c) (Company covenant/obligation breach), 9.01(h) or 9.01(i) (subject to the stated provisos), and within 12 months Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. consummates such proposal or enters a definitive agreement for it (fee payable prior to or concurrently with signing or consummation), in each case payable by same-day wire and not more than once.

Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. shall, and shall cause the Company Subsidiaries to, conduct its business in the ordinary course and use commercially reasonable efforts to (x) preserve intact its present business organization.
This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the Laws of the State of Delaware.
Specific performance is available to Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC, Servier Detroit Inc., Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc., and Servier S.A.S.

In and for any Proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement, each of the parties hereto hereby irrevocably submits to the exclusive jurisdiction and venue of the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware.

Provided that, for purposes of clause (i), none of the following, and no change, event, condition, development, circumstance, state of facts, effect or occurrence that results from or arises in connection with the following, either alone or in combination, shall be deemed to constitute a Company Material Adverse Effect and none of the following shall be taken into account in determining whether there has been a Company Material Adverse Effect: any change, event, condition, development, circumstance, state of facts, effect or occurrence to the extent resulting from or arising in connection with (A) general conditions (or changes therein) in the industry in which the Company and the Company Subsidiaries operate, (B) general economic or regulatory, legislative or political conditions (or changes therein), including any actual or potential stoppage, shutdown, disputed election, default or similar event or occurrence affecting a national or federal government, or securities, credit, banking, financial or other capital markets conditions (including changes generally in prevailing interest rates, currency exchange rates, credit markets or equity price levels or trading volumes), in each case, in the United States, the European Union or elsewhere in the world, (C) any change in applicable Law or GAAP, (D) geopolitical conditions, the outbreak or escalation of hostilities, any acts or threats of war (whether or not declared), sabotage, cyber-intrusion or terrorism, or any escalation or worsening of any of the foregoing, (E) any epidemic, pandemic, disease outbreak or other public health-related event (or escalation or worsening of any such events or occurrences, including, in each case, the response of Governmental Officials thereto), hurricane, tornado, flood, fire, volcano, earthquake or other natural or man-made disaster or any other national or international calamity, crisis or disaster, (F) the failure, in and of itself, of the Company to meet any internal or external projections, forecasts, estimates or predictions in respect of any financial or operating metrics before, on or after the Agreement Date, or changes in the market price or trading volume of the Company Common Stock or the credit rating of the Company (it being understood that the underlying facts giving rise or contributing to such failure or change may be taken into account in determining whether there has been a Company Material Adverse Effect if such facts are not otherwise excluded under this definition), (G) the announcement, pendency or performance of any of the Transactions, including the identity of, or any facts or circumstances relating to, Parent, Merger Sub or their respective Affiliates, any stockholder Proceeding (direct or derivative) in respect of this Agreement or any of the Transactions and any loss of or change in relationship, contractual or otherwise, with any Governmental Entity, supplier, vendor, customer, service provider, collaboration partner, licensor, licensee or any other party having business dealings with the Company or either Company Subsidiary (including the exercise, or prospective exercise, by any party of any rights that arise upon a change of control), or departure of any employees or officers, of the Company or either Company Subsidiary (provided that this clause (G) shall not apply with respect to (1) any representation or warranty contained in Section 4.05 but, in any event, subject to the disclosures set forth in the Company Disclosure Letter otherwise in accordance with this Agreement, (2) any representation or warranty that is intended to address the consequences of the execution, announcement, pendency or performance of this Agreement or the Transactions but, in any event, subject to the disclosures set forth in the Company Disclosure Letter otherwise in accordance with this Agreement, (3) the conditions set forth in clause (ii) or (v) of Exhibit A, solely as any such condition relates to the representation or warranty contained in Section 4.05 or any representation or warranty that expressly addresses the consequences of the execution, announcement, pendency or performance of this Agreement or the Transactions, or (4) any change, event, condition, development, circumstance, state of facts, effect or occurrence arising out of, relating to or with respect to the Company’s performance of Section 6.01), or (H) the Company’s compliance with the covenants contained in this Agreement (other than compliance with Section 6.01), except, in the case of clauses (A), (B), (C), (D) or (E), to the extent that the Company and the Company Subsidiaries, taken as a whole, are disproportionately affected thereby as compared with other participants in the industries in which the Company and the Company Subsidiaries operate (in which case only the incremental disproportionate impact or impacts (to the extent not excluded by other provisions of this definition) may be taken into account in determining whether there has been a Company Material Adverse Effect).

In the “Company Material Adverse Effect” definition, carveouts (A)–(E) apply except to the extent Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. and its subsidiaries are disproportionately affected versus industry participants, in which case only the incremental disproportionate impacts count (Excerpt 1).

Under clause (F), while missing projections or changes in stock price/trading volume or credit rating alone do not constitute a CMAE, the underlying facts giving rise or contributing to such failure/change may be considered, unless otherwise excluded under the definition.
